words.fact: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. helped lead the Civil Rights Movement through organizing, speeches, and nonviolent action.
Rosa Parks became a symbol of resistance after refusing to give up her seat on a segregated bus.
Voting rights were a major focus of civil rights activism.
Marches helped movements gather people, media attention, and momentum.
Civil rights protect people from unfair treatment and discrimination.
Working together gave movements strength and staying power.
Many leaders pushed for change using nonviolent strategies.
Justice means fairness under the law and remains a central goal.
George Washington Carver helped farmers through crop research and agricultural innovation.
Lewis Latimer improved the carbon filament used in light bulbs and held several patents.
A patent protects an inventor's original creation.
Black scientists and inventors have shaped medicine, engineering, agriculture, and more.
STEM stands for science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.
Latimer's work helped make electric lighting more practical.
Every invention starts with an idea worth testing.
Inventors build, test, improve, and try again.
Maya Angelou was a poet and memoirist whose work continues to inspire readers around the world.
James Baldwin wrote powerful essays and novels about race, identity, and justice.
Ella Fitzgerald was one of the most celebrated jazz singers in history.
Duke Ellington was a legendary composer, pianist, and bandleader.
Jazz grew from Black musical traditions and became one of the world's most influential art forms.
Poetry can teach history, identity, courage, and imagination.
Music has often carried hope, protest, and celebration.
Writers preserve stories and challenge people to think in new ways.
Jackie Robinson broke Major League Baseball's modern color barrier in 1947.
Track and field includes many historic Black athletes and record-setters.
Sports fields have often been places where barriers were challenged.
Pioneers often carried responsibility for teammates and community, not just themselves.
Leadership matters on and off the court or field.
Goals reflect effort, focus, and determination.
Sports history includes major breakthroughs in representation and visibility.
Winning matters, but courage can matter even more.
